title: The Effect of a Substituted Amino Acid on Ethylene Biosynthesis, Respiration, Ripening and Preharvest Drop of Apple Fruits1
journal: Journal of the American Society for Horticultural Science
abstract:
Abstract
                  
                    Spraying trees of ‘King of the Pippin’ and ‘Golden Delicious’ (
                    Malus domestica
                    Borkh.) with the substituted amino acid aminoethoxyvinylglycine hydrochloride (AVG), within 1 month of harvest delayed fruit ripening, reduced preharvest drop, and increased fruit removal force (FRF). Five sprays of 5 × 10
                    -4
                    m
                    AVG inhibited the development of the climacteric of ‘Golden Delicious’ apple fruits. Reduced biosynthesis of ethylene was probably responsible for these effects. Naphthaleneacetic acid and AVG synergistically reduced ethylene biosynthesis and warrant further investigation, especially in connection with preharvest drop.
